title: Waveguide connector elements and optical assemblies incorporating the same
abstract: Optical waveguide connector elements for optical coupling optical components of an optical assembly, such as the edge coupling of optical printed circuit boards. In one embodiment, a waveguide connector element includes a first end face and a second end face, a pre-existing optical waveguide within or on a surface of the waveguide connector element, and a laser written optical waveguide optically coupled to an end of the pre-existing optical waveguide and extending toward one of the first end face and the second end face.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A waveguide connector element comprising: a first end face and a second end face; a pre-existing optical waveguide within or on a surface of the waveguide connector element; and a laser written optical waveguide optically coupled to an end of the pre-existing optical waveguide and extending toward one of the first end face and the second end face. 2. The waveguide connector element of claim 1, wherein the laser written optical waveguide has a parallel displacement parallel to the optical printed circuit board and a normal displacement normal to the optical printed circuit board within the waveguide connector element. 3. The waveguide connector element of claims 1 or 2, wherein the pre-existing optical waveguide is one of an ion exchanged optical waveguide, a planar glass dielectric waveguide, an embedded optical fiber waveguide, and a polymer waveguide. 4. The waveguide connector element of any one of claims 1 -3, wherein the pre-existing optical waveguide terminates at one of the first end face and the second end face. 5. The waveguide connector element of claim 4, further comprises a laser bump at one of the first end face and the second end face and aligned with the pre-existing optical waveguide. 6. The waveguide connector element of any one of claims 1 -5, further comprising a beam expanding lens at the first end face, wherein the beam expanding lens is a tapered structure that decreases in diameter from the first end face toward the second end face, the pre-existing optical waveguide extends from the second end face, the laser written optical waveguide optically couples the beam expanding lens to the pre-existing optical waveguide. 7. The waveguide connector element of claim 6, further comprising a laser bump at the first end face and aligned with the beam expanding lens. 8. The waveguide connector element of claim 6, wherein the beam expanding lens is one of an ion exchanged lens, a thermally extending core, and a laser written lens. 9. The waveguide connector element of claim 6, wherein the beam expanding lens is one of a plurality of beam expanding lenses having a first pitch, and the pre-existing optical waveguide is one of a plurality of plurality of pre-existing optical waveguides having a second pitch, and the first pitch is greater than the second pitch. 10. The waveguide connector element of any one of claims 1 -9, further comprising a step feature. 1 1. The waveguide connector element of any one of claims 1 -10, wherein the waveguide connector element is a glass waveguide connector element drawn in a direction perpendicular to an optical axis of the waveguide connector element. 12. The waveguide connector element of claim 1 1, wherein the first end face of the waveguide connector element is curved. 13. The waveguide connector element of any one of claims 1 -12, further comprising a mechanical feature. 14. The waveguide connector element of claim 13, wherein the mechanical feature is at least one of a ridge and a trench. 15. The waveguide connector element of any one of claims 1 -14, further comprising a notch for receiving an optical printed circuit board. 16. A waveguide connector element comprising: a first end face and a second end face; and a beam expanding lens at the first end face, wherein the beam expanding lens is a tapered structure that decreases in diameter from the first end face toward the second end face. 17. The waveguide connector element of claim 16, wherein the beam expanding lens is one of an ion exchanged lens, a thermally extending core, and a laser written lens. 18. The waveguide connector element of claims 16 or 17, further comprising a preexisting waveguide extending from the second end face, wherein the beam expanding lens is directly connected to an end of the pre-existing waveguide. 19. The waveguide connector element of claim 18, wherein the beam expanding lens is a laser written lens. 20. The waveguide connector element of any one of claims 16-19, further comprising a laser written optical waveguide optically coupled to an end of the beam expanding lens and extending toward the second end face, wherein the laser written optical waveguide terminates as a termination location at the second end face. 21. The waveguide connector element of claim 20, wherein the laser written optical waveguide has a parallel displacement in a plane parallel to the optical printed circuit board and a normal displacement in a plane normal to the optical printed circuit board within the waveguide connector element. 22. The waveguide connector element of any one of claims 16-21 , wherein the beam expanding lens terminates at the second end face. 23. The waveguide connector element of claim 22, wherein the beam expanding lens is one of a plurality of beam expanding lenses having a first pitch, and the laser written optical waveguide is one of a plurality of plurality of laser written optical waveguides having a second pitch at the second end face, and the first pitch is greater than the second pitch. 24. The waveguide connector element of any one of claims 16-23, further comprising a step feature. 25. The waveguide connector element of any one of claims 16-24, wherein the waveguide connector element is a glass waveguide connector element drawn in a direction perpendicular to an optical axis of the waveguide connector element. 26. The waveguide connector element of claim 25, wherein the first end face of the waveguide connector element is curved. 27. The waveguide connector element of any one of claims 16-26, further comprising a mechanical feature. 28. The waveguide connector element of claim 27, wherein the mechanical feature is at least one of a ridge and a trench. 29. The waveguide connector element of any one of claims 16-28, further comprising a notch for receiving an optical printed circuit board. 30. An optical assembly comprising: an optical printed circuit board comprising an optical layer and a circuit board optical waveguide disposed within the optical layer, the optical printed circuit board comprising an edge, wherein the circuit board optical waveguide terminates at the edge; and a waveguide connector element comprising an optical waveguide, wherein the waveguide connector element is coupled to the edge of the optical printed circuit board, and the optical waveguide is optically coupled to the circuit board optical waveguide. 31. The optical assembly of claim 30, wherein the optical waveguide comprises a preexisting waveguide proximate a first surface of the waveguide connector element, and a laser written optical waveguide extending from an end of the pre-existing waveguide toward the edge of the optical printed circuit board such that the laser written optical waveguide extends in a direction away from the first surface and toward a center of the waveguide connector element. 32. The optical assembly of claims 30 or 31, wherein: the waveguide connector element comprises a first end face and a second end face; the optical waveguide comprises a pre-existing optical waveguide within the waveguide connector element and extending from the second end face; and the waveguide connector element further comprises a beam expanding lens at the first end face, wherein the beam expanding lens is a tapered structure that decreases in diameter from the first end face toward the second end face. 33. The optical assembly of claim 32, wherein the beam expanding lens is directly connected to the pre-existing optical waveguide. 34. The optical assembly of claim 32, wherein: the beam expanding lens is one of an ion exchanged lens, a thermally extending core, and a laser written lens; and the pre-existing optical waveguide is one of an ion exchanged optical waveguide, a planar glass dielectric waveguide, an embedded optical fiber waveguide, and a polymer waveguide. 35. The optical assembly of claim 30, wherein: the waveguide connector element comprises a first end face and a second end face; and the optical waveguide is defined by a beam expanding lens at the first end face, wherein the beam expanding lens is a tapered structure that decreases in diameter from the first end face toward the second end face such that the beam expanding lens terminates at the second end face and is optically coupled to the circuit board optical waveguide at the second end face. 36. The optical assembly of claim 35, wherein the beam expanding lens is one of an ion exchanged lens, a thermally extending core, and a laser written lens. 37. The optical assembly of claim 30, wherein: the waveguide connector element comprises a first end face, a second end face, and a beam expanding lens at the first end face, wherein the beam expanding lens is a tapered structure that decreases in diameter from the first end face toward the second end face, and the beam expanding lens is directly coupled to the optical waveguide; and the optical waveguide of the waveguide connector element comprises a laser written optical waveguide that extends from the beam expanding lens to the second end face an is optical coupled to the circuit board optical waveguide. 38. The optical assembly of claim 37, wherein the laser written optical waveguide has a parallel displacement in a plane parallel to the optical printed circuit board and a normal displacement in a plane normal to the optical printed circuit board within the waveguide connector element. 39. The optical assembly of claim 37, wherein: the beam expanding lens is one of a plurality of beam expanding lenses having a first pitch; the laser written optical waveguide is one of a plurality of plurality of laser written optical waveguides having a second pitch; the circuit board optical waveguide is one of a plurality of circuit board optical waveguides having the second pitch; and the first pitch is greater than the second pitch. 40. The optical assembly of claim 39, wherein the plurality of laser written optical waveguides transition from the first pitch to the second pitch. 41. The optical assembly of claim 39, wherein the plurality of circuit board optical waveguides is arranged in a one dimensional array. 42. The optical assembly of claim 41 , wherein the plurality of beam expanding lenses is arranged is a one dimensional array. 43. The optical assembly of claim 41 , wherein: the plurality of beam expanding lenses is arranged in a two dimensional array; and the plurality of laser written optical waveguides optically couples the one dimensional array of the plurality of circuit board optical waveguides to the two dimensional array of the plurality of beam expanding lenses. 44. The optical assembly of claim 41 , wherein the plurality of circuit board optical waveguides is arranged in a first plane; and the plurality of beam expanding lenses is arranged in a second plane orthogonal to the first plane. 45. The optical assembly of claim 44, wherein the plurality of beam expanding lenses is arranged above or below the first plane. 46. The optical assembly of claim 44, wherein at least a first portion of the plurality of beam expanding lenses is arranged above the first plane and at least a second portion of the plurality of beam expanding lenses is arranged below the first plane. 47. The optical assembly of claim 46, wherein the plurality of beam expanding lenses is arranged in a two dimensional array. 48. The optical assembly of any one of claims 30-47, wherein: the circuit board optical waveguide comprises a pre-existing optical waveguide proximate a first surface of the optical layer and a laser written optical waveguide coupled to an end of the pre-existing optical waveguide and extending to the edge of the optical printed circuit board; and the laser written optical waveguide extends in a direction away from the first surface and toward a center of the optical layer. 49. The optical assembly of claim 48, wherein: 

 the pre-existing optical waveguide is one of a plurality of pre-existing optical waveguides arranged at a first pitch; the laser written optical waveguide is one of a plurality of laser written optical waveguides; the plurality of laser written optical waveguides is arranged at the first pitch at the end of the plurality of pre-existing optical waveguides and is arranged at a second pitch at the edge of the optical printed circuit board; and the first pitch is smaller than the second pitch. 50. The optical assembly of claim 30, wherein the waveguide connector element is a glass waveguide connector element drawn in a direction perpendicular to an optical axis of the waveguide connector element. 51. The optical assembly of claim 50, wherein a first end face of the waveguide connector element is curved. 52. The optical assembly of claim 30, wherein the waveguide connector element is coupled to the edge of the optical printed circuit board by an adhesive. 53. The optical assembly of claim 30, further comprising a housing operable to couple the waveguide connector element to the edge of the optical printed circuit board. 54. The optical assembly of claim 53, wherein the waveguide connector element is secured within the housing by at least one insert and an adhesive. 55. The optical assembly of any one of claims 30-54, wherein the waveguide connector element comprises a step feature. 56. The optical assembly of claim 55, wherein: the optical printed circuit board comprises a dielectric layer coupled to the optical layer; and a surface of the step feature is coupled to a surface of the dielectric layer. 57. The optical assembly of claim 55, wherein: the optical printed circuit board comprises a dielectric layer coupled to the optical layer; the dielectric layer comprises a cutout exposing a first surface of the optical layer; and a surface of the step feature is coupled to the first surface of the dielectric layer. 58. The optical assembly of claim 30, wherein the waveguide connector element comprises a notch, and the optical printed circuit board is disposed within the notch such that the edge of the optical printed circuit board is coupled to the waveguide connector element. 59. The optical assembly of claim 30, wherein: the waveguide connector element comprises a first end face, a second end face, and a beam expanding lens at the first end face, wherein the beam expanding lens is a tapered structure that decreases in diameter from the first end face toward the second end face, and the beam expanding lens is directly coupled to the optical waveguide; 

 the optical waveguide of the waveguide connector element comprises a laser written optical waveguide that extends from the beam expanding lens to the second end face an is optical coupled to the circuit board optical waveguide; the beam expanding lens is one of a plurality of beam expanding lenses; the laser written optical waveguide is one of a plurality of plurality of laser written optical waveguides; the plurality of beam expanding lenses is arranged in a two dimensional array; the waveguide connector element comprises a step feature; a portion of the plurality of laser written optical waveguides are disposed within the step feature; the optical printed circuit board comprises a flexible optical layer comprising a plurality of flexible circuit board optical waveguides, and a first dielectric layer coupled to the optical layer; a surface of the step feature is coupled to a surface of the first dielectric layer; the circuit board optical waveguide is one of a plurality of circuit board optical waveguides; the plurality of circuit board optical waveguides of the optical layer is optically coupled to a first portion of the plurality of laser written optical waveguides; and the flexible optical layer is deflected away from a plane defined by the optical printed circuit board such that the plurality of flexible circuit board optical waveguides are optically coupled to the portion of the plurality of laser written optical waveguides disposed within the step feature. 60. The optical assembly of claim 30, wherein: 

 the waveguide connector element comprises a first end face, a second end face, and a beam expanding lens at the first end face, wherein the beam expanding lens is a tapered structure that decreases in diameter from the first end face toward the second end face, and the beam expanding lens is directly coupled to the optical waveguide; and the optical waveguide of the waveguide connector element comprises a laser written optical waveguide that extends from the beam expanding lens to the second end face an is optical coupled to the circuit board optical waveguide; the beam expanding lens is one of a plurality of beam expanding lenses; the laser written optical waveguide is one of a plurality of plurality of laser written optical waveguides; the plurality of beam expanding lenses is arranged in a two dimensional array; the waveguide connector element comprises a notch; the optical printed circuit board is disposed within the notch; the optical printed circuit board comprises a first flexible optical layer and a second flexible optical layer each comprising a plurality of flexible circuit board optical waveguides, a first dielectric layer coupled to the optical layer, and a second dielectric layer; the circuit board optical waveguide is one of a plurality of circuit board optical waveguides; the plurality of circuit board optical waveguides of the optical layer is optically coupled to a first portion of the plurality of laser written optical waveguides; the first flexible optical layer is deflected away from a plane defined by the optical printed circuit board and away from the first dielectric layer such that the plurality of flexible circuit board optical waveguides within the first flexible optical layer are 

 optically coupled to a second portion of the plurality of laser written optical waveguides; and the second flexible optical layer is deflected away from the plane defined by the optical printed circuit board and away from the second dielectric layer such that the plurality of second flexible circuit board optical waveguides within the second flexible optical layer are optically coupled to a third portion of the plurality of laser written optical waveguides. 61. An optical assembly comprising: a waveguide connector element comprising: a first end face and a second end face; a plurality of pre-existing optical waveguides within or on a surface of the waveguide connector element, the plurality of pre-existing optical waveguides arranged at a first pitch and extending from the second end face; and a plurality of laser written optical waveguides optically coupled to an end of the plurality of pre-existing optical waveguides and extending toward the first end face, the plurality of laser written optical waveguides configured as fan-out optical waveguides such that the plurality of laser written optical waveguides is arranged at a second pitch at the first end face, wherein the second pitch is greater than the first pitch; and a plurality of optical fibers optically coupled to the plurality of laser written optical waveguides at the first end face. 62. The optical assembly of claim 61 , wherein the plurality of optical fibers is disposed within an optical fiber connector. 63. The optical assembly of claims 61 or 62, wherein the laser written optical waveguide has a parallel displacement in a plane parallel to the optical printed circuit board and a normal displacement in a plane normal to the optical printed circuit board within the waveguide connector element. 64. An optical assembly comprising: an optical connector element comprising: a first end face and a second end face; a plurality of pre-existing optical waveguides within or on a surface of the optical connector element, the plurality of pre-existing optical waveguides arranged at a first pitch and extending from the second end face; and a plurality of laser written optical waveguides optically coupled to an end of the plurality of pre-existing optical waveguides and extending toward one the first end face, the plurality of laser written optical waveguides configured as fan-out optical waveguides such that the plurality of laser written optical waveguides is arranged at a second pitch at the first end face, wherein the second pitch is greater than the first pitch; and an active optical component coupled to a surface of the optical connector element and optically coupled to the plurality of laser written optical waveguides. 65. The optical assembly of claim 64, wherein the surface is the first end face of the optical connector element.
